text: Title: THE OPPOSITION AGAINST THE MARRIAGE CONTRACT OF LGBT COUPLES AS A MARRIAGE IN ISLAMIC FAMILY LAW BASED ON MAZLAHAH THEORY Background: Recently, there has been a lot of discussion regarding the marriage contract of LGBT couples as a marriage in Islamic family law, with many Muslims opposing it. From the view of Islamic family law, they consider the opposition as a correct and just decision. Based on Maslahah theory, the purpose of the law is to fulfill the benefits of the society. Methods: The research method used is literature study with qualitative approach using Maslahah scalpel. Discussion: Marriage in Islam is not a contract between two individuals but also it is an institution with conditions and pillars. Some of the conditions and pillars of marriage in Islam are such as the existence of male and female parties that are allowed in Sharia, a guardian, dowry, and fair witnesses. When LGBT couples enter into a marriage contract, the marriage contract does not fulfill those conditions. Therefore, the marriage contract of LGBT couples cannot be called a marriage contract in Islamic family law. In addition, the opposition against the marriage contract of LGBT couples as a marriage in Islamic family law can be explained by using Maslahah theory. Maslahah theory itself is used to determine and know the benefit of the law. In Islam, the law that is considered to give benefits to mankind is called as Maslahah Al Mushlaahah, which consists of four basic components. Those are, the protection of the structure of the family institution, the upholding of morality, and the realization of the goal of religion. Thus, the opposition against the marriage contract of LGBT couples as a marriage in Islamic family law is to maintain the Maslahah Al Mushlaahah which will bring a greater benefit to the society, such as the preservation of the social harmony, and upholding the laws of Islam. Conclusion: Islamic law is dynamic law that has the ability to accommodate changes of time, while still maintaining the justice and benefit of the society. Based on the research, it can be concluded that the opposition against the marriage contract of LGBT couples as a marriage in Islamic family law is not discrimination, but it is a deeper understanding of marriage in Islam and to fulfill the welfare and the benefit of the society, especially in preserving the institution of family as the basic social and spiritual institution in the society.
